atlas.experiment: e031 causal action smearing (H008 obstacle + partial cure)
atlas.put_in: flat 2D causal-diamond sprinklings; the raw 2D BD action and its mesoscale-smeared version
emerged: raw BD action std vs N: [(300, 81.53), (600, 228.34), (1200, 285.43)] (grows -> fluctuation-dominated)
smeared BD action std vs N: [(300, 10.91), (600, 23.62), (1200, 40.88)] (damped ~6.98x at largest N)
curvature FLOOR: flat=9.96+-40.88 vs density-bump=26.49+-21.59 (only marginal separation)
surprises: the raw action's fluctuations GROW with N (no clean value); mesoscale smearing suppresses them by ~an order of magnitude
atlas.persistence: raw fluctuations grow with N; the smeared action is far more stable at every N
atlas.not_scripted_check: the fluctuation growth and its suppression are measured from the sprinklings
atlas.claim_tier: measured (raw fluctuations grow; smearing damps >5x) ; interpretive (the causal-set action needs mesoscale smearing to be usable -- H008 quantified) ; analogy (CS action) ; FRONTIER
floors: FRONTIER: clean curvature recovery is NOT achieved at accessible N (curvature bump only ~1 sigma above flat) = reported floor; the deep frontier stays open
the result is the quantitative OBSTACLE (raw fluctuates) + PARTIAL cure (smearing damps)
'causal-set action / curvature' is analogy for the measured action statistics
